---
title: "Untitled"
author: "miryala vaishnavi"
date: "2026-03-10"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
base <- getwd()
path <- file.path(base, "data_brsm", "single", "lab")
files <- sort(list.files(path = path,
                         pattern = "\\.csv$",
                         full.names = TRUE))

single_lab_rt <- c()
single_lab_acc <- c()
single_lab_fr <- c()
single_levelwise_rt <- NULL

for (file in files) {
  
  df <- read.csv(file)
  entries <- df$mouse.clicked_name
  entries <- entries[!is.na(entries)]
  
  all_clicks <- c()
  for (entry in entries) {
    
    clean <- gsub("\\[|\\]|'", "", entry)
    clicks <- strsplit(clean, ",")[[1]]
    clicks <- trimws(clicks)
    
    all_clicks <- c(all_clicks, clicks)
  }
  
  total_clicks <- length(all_clicks)
  hits <- sum(all_clicks == "target")
  false_clicks <- total_clicks - hits
  
  accuracy <- hits / total_clicks
  false_alarm_rate <- false_clicks / total_clicks
  
  single_lab_acc <- c(single_lab_acc, accuracy)
  single_lab_fr  <- c(single_lab_fr, false_alarm_rate)
  response_times <- df$mouse.time
  response_times <- response_times[!is.na(response_times)]
  response_times <- gsub("\\[|\\]", "", response_times)
  response_times <- as.numeric(response_times)

  single_levelwise_rt <- rbind(single_levelwise_rt, response_times)
  single_lab_rt <- c(single_lab_rt,
                          mean(response_times, na.rm = TRUE))
}

print(mean(single_lab_rt))

path1 <- file.path(getwd(), "data_brsm", "multiple", "lab")

files <- sort(list.files(path = path1,
                         pattern = "\\.csv$",
                         full.names = TRUE))

multi_lab_rt <- c()
multi_lab_acc <- c()
multi_levelwise_rt <- NULL

for (file in files) {
  
  df <- read.csv(file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # ----- RT calculation -----
  
  all_values <- c()
  entries <- df$mouse.time
  entries <- entries[!is.na(entries)]
  for (entry in entries) {
    
    split_lists <- strsplit(gsub("\\]\\[", "]|||[", entry), "\\|\\|\\|")[[1]]
    
    for (lst in split_lists) {
      
      clean <- gsub("\\[|\\]", "", lst)
      
      if (nchar(clean) > 0) {
        parsed <- as.numeric(strsplit(clean, ",")[[1]])
        
        if (length(parsed) > 0) {
          s <- tail(parsed, 1)
          all_values <- c(all_values, s / length(parsed))
        }
      }
    }
  }
  multi_levelwise_rt <- rbind(multi_levelwise_rt, all_values)
  multi_lab_rt <- c(multi_lab_rt, mean(all_values))
  
  
  # ----- Accuracy calculation -----
  
  entries1 <- df$mouse.clicked_name
  entries1 <- entries1[!is.na(entries1)]
  
  round_acc <- c()
  
  for (entry in entries1) {
    
    clean <- gsub("\\[|\\]|'", "", entry)
    clicks <- strsplit(clean, ",")[[1]]
    clicks <- trimws(clicks)
    
    unique_targets <- length(unique(clicks))
    
    acc <- unique_targets / 5
    
    round_acc <- c(round_acc, acc)
  }
  
  multi_lab_acc <- c(multi_lab_acc, mean(round_acc))
}

hist(single_lab_rt,
     main = "Single Lab - Mean RT",
     probability = TRUE,
     ylim = c(0, 3),
     xlab = "Mean Reaction Time",
     col = "lightblue",
     border = "black")
lines(density(single_lab_rt),
      col = "blue",
      lwd = 2)

hist(multi_lab_rt,
     main = "Multiple Lab - Mean RT",
     ylim = c(0, 3),
     probability = TRUE,
     xlab = "Mean Reaction Time",
     col = "lightgreen",
     border = "black")
lines(density(multi_lab_rt),
      col = "darkgreen",
      lwd = 2)

boxplot(single_lab_rt, multi_lab_rt,
        names = c("Single Target", "Multi Target"),
        main = "Lab Reaction Time Comparison",
        ylab = "Mean Reaction Time",
        col = c("lightblue", "lightgreen"),
        border = "black")

print(sd(single_lab_rt))
print(sd(multi_lab_rt))
print(mean(multi_lab_acc))
print(sd(multi_lab_acc))
single_levelwise_rt <- colMeans(single_levelwise_rt, na.rm = TRUE)
print(single_levelwise_rt, na.rm = TRUE)
print(multi_levelwise_rt)







library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

multi_lab_inter <- lab_trials %>%
  filter(Condition == "Multiple",
         !is.na(InterTarget_ms),
         InterTarget_ms > 0) %>%
  group_by(Level) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean_Inter = mean(InterTarget_ms, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_Inter   = sd(InterTarget_ms,   na.rm = TRUE),
    n_trials   = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

multi_lab_inter   # this is the table you report

y_rng  <- range(multi_lab_inter$Mean_Inter, na.rm = TRUE)
y_pad  <- 0.1 * diff(y_rng)
y_lims <- y_rng + c(-y_pad, y_pad)
x_max  <- max(multi_lab_inter$Level)

ggplot(multi_lab_inter, aes(x = Level, y = Mean_Inter)) +
  geom_line(color = "darkorange") +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:x_max,
                     limits = c(0.5, x_max + 0.5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = y_lims,
                     expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0))) +
  labs(
    title = "Average Inter-Target Time Across Levels (Multiple - Lab)",
    x = "Level / Trial",
    y = "Average Inter-Target Time (ms)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()






library(dplyr)

# Game (phone)
fa_game_summary <- phone_trials %>%
  group_by(Condition) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_FA = mean(FalseAlarms, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_FA   = sd(FalseAlarms,   na.rm = TRUE),
    n       = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

fa_game_summary

# Lab
fa_lab_summary <- lab_trials %>%
  group_by(Condition) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_FA = mean(FalseAlarms, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_FA   = sd(FalseAlarms,   na.rm = TRUE),
    n       = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

fa_lab_summary

# Optional: nice tables in the report
knitr::kable(fa_game_summary, caption = "False Alarms – Game")
knitr::kable(fa_lab_summary,  caption = "False Alarms – Lab")







library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# Participant-level mean RT in each modality
subj_phone_rt <- phone_trials %>%
  group_by(Participant, Condition) %>%
  summarise(Game_RT = mean(RT_ms, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

subj_lab_rt <- lab_trials %>%
  group_by(Participant, Condition) %>%
  summarise(Lab_RT = mean(RT_ms, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

subj_rt <- inner_join(subj_phone_rt, subj_lab_rt,
                      by = c("Participant", "Condition"))

# ----- Single Target -----
single_long <- subj_rt %>%
  filter(Condition == "Single") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Game_RT, Lab_RT),
               names_to = "Modality",
               values_to = "RT_ms") %>%
  mutate(Modality = ifelse(Modality == "Game_RT", "Game", "Lab"))

# line plot
ggplot(single_long,
       aes(x = Modality, y = RT_ms, group = Participant, color = Participant)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Reaction Time Comparison (Single Target: Game vs Lab)",
    x = "Modality",
    y = "Mean RT per Participant (ms)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# boxplot
ggplot(single_long,
       aes(x = Modality, y = RT_ms, fill = Modality)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Reaction Time Comparison (Single Target: Game vs Lab)",
    x = "Modality",
    y = "Mean RT per Participant (ms)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ----- Multiple Target -----
multiple_long <- subj_rt %>%
  filter(Condition == "Multiple") %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(Game_RT, Lab_RT),
               names_to = "Modality",
               values_to = "RT_ms") %>%
  mutate(Modality = ifelse(Modality == "Game_RT", "Game", "Lab"))

ggplot(multiple_long,
       aes(x = Modality, y = RT_ms, group = Participant, color = Participant)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(
    title = "Reaction Time Comparison (Multiple Target: Game vs Lab)",
    x = "Modality",
    y = "Mean RT per Participant (ms)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

ggplot(multiple_long,
       aes(x = Modality, y = RT_ms, fill = Modality)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(
    title = "Reaction Time Comparison (Multiple Target: Game vs Lab)",
    x = "Modality",
    y = "Mean RT per Participant (ms)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()








library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# subj_rt is already created in the previous chunk

# ----- Single -----
single_corr <- subj_rt %>% filter(Condition == "Single")

ggplot(single_corr, aes(x = Lab_RT, y = Game_RT)) +
  geom_point(color = "steelblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "darkred") +
  labs(
    title = "Game RT vs Lab RT (Single Target)",
    x = "Lab Mean RT (ms)",
    y = "Game Mean RT (ms)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

cat("Single – Pearson r:",
    cor(single_corr$Lab_RT, single_corr$Game_RT,
        use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson"),
    "\n")

cat("Single – Spearman rho:",
    cor(single_corr$Lab_RT, single_corr$Game_RT,
        use = "complete.obs", method = "spearman"),
    "\n")

# ----- Multiple -----
multiple_corr <- subj_rt %>% filter(Condition == "Multiple")

ggplot(multiple_corr, aes(x = Lab_RT, y = Game_RT)) +
  geom_point(color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, color = "darkred") +
  labs(
    title = "Game RT vs Lab RT (Multiple Targets)",
    x = "Lab Mean RT (ms)",
    y = "Game Mean RT (ms)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

cat("Multiple – Pearson r:",
    cor(multiple_corr$Lab_RT, multiple_corr$Game_RT,
        use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson"),
    "\n")

cat("Multiple – Spearman rho:",
    cor(multiple_corr$Lab_RT, multiple_corr$Game_RT,
        use = "complete.obs", method = "spearman"),
    "\n")




```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
